action.reasoning: Observation: Best accuracy 21.32% with CLIP ViT-B/16 + SWAD. Multiple AugReg training attempts failed due to truncated scripts causing DDP crashes (syntax errors in incomplete files). Analysis: Root cause was truncated train_augreg.py. Using DataParallel instead of DDP eliminates the need for torchrun and multi-process setup, making it much more reliable. Ensembling diverse backbones (CLIP vision-language + AugReg supervised IN-1k) should improve OOD generalization on Quickdraw by combining different inductive biases. Decision: Write complete, concise train_augreg.py using DataParallel (single process, multi-GPU), train AugReg ViT-B/16 for 8 epochs with SWAD + mixup + strong augmentation, then submit eval with ensemble in next iteration.
